basic_details.job_title: UKMSSB Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 - 196 Vacancies, Apply Online by 2 July 2026
basic_details.short_information: The Uttarakhand Medical Service Selection Board (UKMSSB) has released an official notification (Advertisement No. UKMSSB/Exam/16/2025-26/448, dated 3 June 2026) for the recruitment of 196 Nursing Officers (Male/Female) - both diploma and degree holders - under the Medical Health and Family Welfare Department, Uttarakhand. Online applications are open from 12 June 2026 and the last date to apply is 2 July 2026 by 5:00 PM. The written examination is tentatively scheduled for November–December 2026.

application_fee.details: The application fee must be paid online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. The fee is non-refundable under any circumstances, including if the application is cancelled by the candidate after payment. | Ex-Servicemen from Uttarakhand must pay the fee applicable to their own category (General/SC/ST/OBC as applicable). Fee once paid will not be refunded if the application is later cancelled by the candidate to re-apply after correction.

qualification.details: All four post codes require a nursing degree or diploma from a recognized institution affiliated with the Indian Nursing Council, along with valid registration. Candidates must verify they hold the correct qualification category (diploma or degree) before applying, as post codes differ for each. | For Diploma Holder Posts (Post Codes 801 & 803), a candidate must possess one of the following: | B.Sc. (Honours) in Nursing from a university/institution recognised by the Indian Nursing Council (INC), or B.Sc. Nursing (regular course) from an INC-recognised institution, or Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing from an INC-recognised institution, or Diploma in General Nursing and Midwifery/Psychiatric Nursing from an INC-recognised university | For Degree Holder Posts (Post Codes 802 & 804), the same set of qualifications above applies. The classification as "diploma holder" or "degree holder" in the post code is based on the nature of the original nursing programme - candidates should refer to the official notification for the exact distinction applicable to their qualification. | Additionally, candidates for all posts must have: | Working knowledge of Hindi Valid/renewed permanent registration certificate from Uttarakhand Nurses and Midwives Council, Dehradun, as on the last date of online application (2 July 2026). The NUI card or receipt of application for registration is not valid - only the permanent registration certificate from Uttarakhand Nurses and Midwives Council is accepted | Desirable Qualification (either one): | Minimum 2 years of service in the Territorial Army, or 'B' or 'C' certificate from National Cadet Corps (NCC) | The age cut-off date for this recruitment is 1 July 2025. Candidates must be at least 21 years and must not have exceeded 42 years of age as on 1 July 2025. | Candidates wondering about age for SC/ST/OBC should note that the relaxation is given cumulatively with only one other permissible sub-category. Ex-Servicemen from Uttarakhand may deduct their period of military service from their actual age - if the resulting age does not exceed the prescribed maximum by more than 3 years, they are deemed to have met the upper age condition. | The benefit of reservation - both vertical (SC/ST/OBC/EWS) and horizontal (PwBD, Ex-Servicemen, Freedom Fighter Dependents, Orphans, State Agitators/Dependents, Skilled Sportspersons) - is available only to permanent residents/domiciles of Uttarakhand State. Candidates not domiciled in Uttarakhand will not receive any reservation benefit even if they belong to a reserved category. Candidates must hold a valid Uttarakhand domicile certificate issued by the competent authority by the last date of application. | For OBC candidates: the OBC certificate must have been issued no earlier than 2 July 2023 (i.e., not more than 3 years before the last date of application, 2 July 2026), and must conform to the conditions of Government Order No. 310/XVII-2/16-02(OBC)/2012 dated 26 February 2016. OBC candidates falling under the creamy layer are not eligible for OBC reservation. | For EWS candidates: the Income and Asset Certificate must be valid for Financial Year 2026–27. No age relaxation is available to EWS candidates.

salary_details.details: The selected Nursing Officers will be placed in Pay Level 7 under the 7th Pay Commission. This is a non-gazetted post with a contributory pension scheme (Contributory Pension). | The post is non-gazetted and carries a contributory pension. The pay of selected candidates will be fixed as per prevailing government rules.

selection_process: The selection process for UKMSSB Nursing Officer 2026 follows the provisions of the Uttarakhand Subordinate Nursing (Non-Gazetted) Service Rules
2015 and subsequent amendments. Selection is based entirely on a written competitive examination with no interview stage.
The objective-type written exam is divided into two papers:
as well as national and international geography

Negative Marking: 1/4 mark is deducted for every wrong answer. No marks are deducted for unattempted questions. Minimum Qualifying Marks: General/OBC/EWS candidates must score at least 45% overall; SC/ST candidates must score at least 35%. Question Booklet Language: Both Hindi and English. Answer Sheet: OMR (triplicate) - the original and second copy are deposited with the invigilator; the third copy (carbon) may be taken by the candidate. Question Booklet: Candidates are permitted to take the question booklet home after the exam. Answer Key: Displayed on www.ukmssb.org after the examination. Candidates may raise objections within 7 days of answer key publication by paying ₹50 per question objection fee through the online portal. Exam Centres (Four Cities): Dehradun

Almora. Candidates must select two preferred centres. The Board may change the allotted centre based on candidate numbers. Calculators and electronic devices are strictly prohibited in the examination hall. Mobile phones
or any communication device are not permitted. Tie-breaking rule: If two candidates score equally
priority is given first to the candidate with a higher desirable qualification
then alphabetically by name in English.

Candidates shortlisted based on written test merit will undergo document verification and mandatory medical examination before final appointment. Final selection and appointment is provisional until full verification of eligibility is completed.

The written examination is tentatively scheduled for November–December 2026. Candidates can receive 50% bus fare concession on Uttarakhand Transport Corporation buses for travel two days before the exam and up to two days after - on producing the admit card and Uttarakhand domicile certificate copy to the bus conductor.
how_to_apply: Applications are accepted only online at www.ukmssb.org. No offline or hard-copy applications are accepted. The five-step application process is as follows:

Register: Visit www.ukmssb.org → click "Apply Now" → click "Candidate Register Here." Complete registration using a valid email ID and mobile number. Choose the correct post code and post name carefully at this stage.

Log In and Fill Application: Use the Registration Number and Password received on your email/mobile to log in. Fill in all personal details, educational qualification, council registration details (Registration Number, date, and validity date of Uttarakhand Nurses and Midwives Council registration), and select two examination centre cities from: Dehradun, Srinagar, Haldwani, Almora.

Upload Documents: Upload a recent passport-size photograph (20 KB – 50 KB, JPG format) and signature (5 KB – 20 KB, JPG format). Upload a Combined Single PDF File (200 KB – 2 MB) containing the following documents: High School Certificate (mandatory) Intermediate Certificate (mandatory) Uttarakhand Domicile Certificate or Non-transferable Job Certificate (mandatory if applicable) Category Certificate SC/ST/OBC/EWS (if applicable) Sub-category certificates: DFF/Ex-Army/PwBD/Orphan/State Agitator/Skilled Sportsperson (if applicable) All year/semester mark sheets of Degree/Diploma (mandatory) Degree/Diploma Passing Certificate (mandatory) NCC/Territorial Army Certificate (if applicable) Uttarakhand Nurses and Midwives Council Registration Certificate (mandatory)
Uttarakhand Domicile Certificate or Non-transferable Job Certificate (mandatory if applicable)
Sub-category certificates: DFF/Ex-Army/PwBD/Orphan/State Agitator/Skilled Sportsperson (if applicable)
All year/semester mark sheets of Degree/Diploma (mandatory)
Uttarakhand Nurses and Midwives Council Registration Certificate (mandatory)
Pay the Fee: Pay the applicable application fee online through Net Banking, Debit Card, Credit Card, or UPI. Save the payment receipt. Applications without completed fee payment will be rejected.
Download and Save Application: After successful payment, download and keep a printed copy of the application form for future reference.

After the application closes on 2 July 2026, a one-time correction window opens from 4 July 2026 to 7 July 2026 (by 5:00 PM). Candidates may log in with their Registration Number and Password to correct most details - except mobile number and email ID. If a candidate changes their category/sub-category during correction, the extra fee (if any) paid for the previous category will not be refunded.

faq.question: Q1. What is the UKMSSB Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 notification date?
faq.answer: The Uttarakhand Medical Service Selection Board (UKMSSB) released the Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 notification on 03-06-2026.

faq.question: Q2. How many Nursing Officer vacancies are available under UKMSSB 2026?
faq.answer: There are a total of 196 Nursing Officer posts, including male and female candidates with diploma and degree qualifications.

faq.question: Q3. What are the eligibility criteria for UKMSSB Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must possess GNM, B.Sc. (Hons.) Nursing, B.Sc. Nursing, or Post Basic B.Sc. Nursing degrees.

faq.question: Q4. What is the age limit for UKMSSB Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Applicants should be between 21 and 42 years. Age relaxation is applicable as per government rules.

faq.question: Q5. What is the application fee for UKMSSB Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: General/OBC: Rs. 300 SC/ST/EWS/PWD: Rs. 150 Payment is to be made online.

faq.question: Q6. What is the UKMSSB Nursing Officer pay scale 2026?
faq.answer: The salary is Rs. 44,900 – Rs. 1,42,400 (Level-7) as per the 7th CPC pay matrix.

faq.question: Q7. How can I apply online for UKMSSB Nursing Officer 2026?
faq.answer: Candidates must apply through the official UKMSSB website: ukmssb.org. Ensure eligibility before submitting the application.

faq.question: Q8. Where can I download the UKMSSB Nursing Officer Recruitment 2026 notification PDF?
faq.answer: The official notification PDF can be downloaded directly from UKMSSB official website under the Recruitment section.
